title: Nurse
description: Nurses are healthcare professionals who provide direct patient care, administer medications, monitor vital signs, educate patients and families, and collaborate with other healthcare team members to promote health and well-being.
content: Nursing involves a combination of scientific knowledge, practical skills, and compassionate care. Nurses work in various settings, including hospitals, clinics, community health centers, and private practices. They play a vital role in patient recovery, disease prevention, and health promotion. In India, nurses are increasingly recognized for their critical role in the healthcare system, especially in rural areas where access to doctors may be limited. They often require a Bachelor of Science in Nursing (B.Sc Nursing) or a General Nursing and Midwifery (GNM) diploma.

financialFeasibility.note: Nursing education is relatively affordable, especially with government-subsidized programs. Job opportunities are plentiful, making it a financially viable career path for individuals from diverse socioeconomic backgrounds. However, private nursing colleges can be expensive.

sectorScore.remarks: Both government and private hospitals offer nursing positions. Private hospitals often offer better salaries and benefits, while government jobs provide greater job security and pension benefits.

relatedCareers.reason: Nurses work closely with doctors to provide patient care and implement treatment plans.
relatedCareers.title: Doctor
courses: M.B.B.S - Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery

relatedCareers.reason: Nurses administer medications prescribed by pharmacists patient responses.
relatedCareers.title: Pharmacist

relatedCareers.reason: Nurses often collaborate with medical technicians to perform diagnostic tests and procedures.
relatedCareers.title: Medical Technician

genderDistribution.note: Nursing has traditionally been a female-dominated profession in India, but the number of male nurses is gradually increasing.
